title: All iPhone 18 models and the iPhone Air 2 are now rumored to get this improvement
description: Apple was long rumored to get rid of the Dynamic Island on the iPhone 18 Pro and iPhone 18 Pro Max, but recently a new rumor claimed that wouldn't happen, and instead, they would just have a smaller Dynamic Island. Today, a new rumor from Ice Universe in China brings some more context. According to him, it's not just the Pros that will get the smaller Dynamic Island - but the entire series, including the iPhone 18 and the iPhone Air 2. On the other hand, the screen bezels will stay exactly the same as they are in the iPhone 17, iPhone 17 Pro, iPhone 17 Pro Max, and iPhone Air,...

title: Honor starts teasing the 600 and 600 Pro, promises a "next-gen camera"
description: A few days ago, the upcoming Honor 600 and Honor 600 Pro leaked in official-looking renders, and in the meantime the former was also spotted on Geekbench rocking the Snapdragon 7 Gen 4 SoC. Now, Honor has officially started teasing the duo in Malaysia, where it's put up a special microsite for their upcoming launch. Three themes are teased as you can see in the images below: "Next Design", "Next AI", and "Next-Gen Camera". Honor 600 series teaser images The design is, according to the aforementioned leaked renders, heavily inspired by the iPhone 17 Pro and iPhone 17 Pro Max,...

title: The new Xiaomi Redmi A7 Pro has a massive 6.9” display and a 6,000mAh battery
description: Xiaomi quietly released the affordable Redmi A7 Pro – it’s currently available in Italy and Indonesia. This phone is very similar to the Redmi A5 and Poco C71, except (as far as we can tell) it runs full-fat Android 16 / HyperOS 3 instead of the lighter Go Edition like the other two models. The A7 Pro is powered by the same Unisoc T7250 chipset, a rather old 12nm part. The more worrying spec is the 4GB of LPDDR4X RAM – that’s not a lot for full Android and apps (Go Edition apps are lighter). For storage, you can have either 64GB or 128GB of UFS 2.2. The good news is that there’s a microSD...

title: Samsung Galaxy S26 with 128GB storage exists but you probably don't want it
description: A little over a week after debuting its Galaxy S26 Ultra Enterprise Edition, Samsung has seemingly also introduced a corporate-exclusive version of the Galaxy S26. The more notable bit is that we’re seeing two versions of the S26 Enterprise Edition, with one featuring 256GB storage and retailing for £899 and a second option with 128GB storage with a retail price of £660. The 128GB storage version is pretty weird considering the regular Galaxy S26 starts with 256GB storage. The £239 lower starting price is pretty noteworthy too, but it's still not a regular phone that you can just buy...

title: Gemini can now import your chat history from other AI chatbots
description: AI chatbots have become an intrinsic part of our online activity, and as usual, there are different camps. Some use ChatGPT while others prefer Claude or Google's Gemini. These chatbots are programmed to learn from your behavior, interests and past interactions to serve you more relevant information and answers. So if you decide to switch from one AI service to another, you'd have to start from scratch, as your chat history won't be available. Google is making the switch much easier with the latest update to Gemini. The company has now added a new Import memory tool that lets you...

title: Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra vs. Galaxy S25 Ultra
description: The Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra is here, and for yet another year, we get a rather modest upgrade over the previous generation. And at this point, it has become an age-old question: should you go for the newer Galaxy or the older Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra will do just fine? With Samsung's long software support promise of 7 years of OS upgrades and security patches, the latest generation has one fewer advantage over the older one, so even if you choose to go for the S25 Ultra, you won't be missing out on important software upgrades. Despite the lack of proper hardware upgrades, the Galaxy...

title: Tecno Spark 50 5G debuts with a 6,500mAh battery, Dimensity 6400 SoC
description: As expected, Tecno has unveiled its latest Spark series smartphone in India. The Spark 50 is a 5G phone that comes with a 6,500mAh battery and a Dimensity 6400 chipset. It will be available for purchase starting April 3. The handset comes with a 6.78-inch display with an HD+ resolution and a refresh rate of 120Hz. It’s an LCD panel that offers up to 560 nits brightness and 240Hz touch sampling rate. You get a 6nm octa-core MediaTek Dimensity 6400 SoC with a Mali-G57 MC2 GPU. The phone can be configured with up to 6GB LPDDR4x RAM and 128GB UFS 2.2 storage. There’s a microSD card slot...

title: The Ultrahuman Ring Pro is now available for pre-order in the US, here's how to get a 10% discount
description: The Ultrahuman Ring Pro was announced at the end of February and went on pre-order around the world with the notable exception of the US. But now the red tape hurdles have been cleared and the Pro ring is available for pre-order in the US as well. The Ultrahuman Ring Pro plus the Pro Charging Case is sold as a bundle priced at $479. This is an early bird pricing tier that applies only to a limited number of units – after those sell out, the price will go up. We have been covering Ultrahuman for a long while now, so they sent us an exclusive code that our readers can use to get a 10%...

title: Redmi 15A arrives with familiar specs
description: The newest addition to the Redmi 15 series was just unveiled in India. Say hello to the Redmi 15A, which arrives in India with a 6,300mAh battery, a 120Hz display and affordable pricing. It’s actually a rebadged Poco C85x with a different camera island design. Redmi 15A design and key specs Redmi 15A is built around a 6.9-inch IPS LCD with HD+ resolution and a 120Hz refresh rate. The panel is rated at 810 nits peak brightness and features an 8MP selfie cam. The back houses a 32MP main shooter and an unspecified auxiliary lens. You get a Unisoc T8300 chipset at the helm paired with...

title: Android 17 Beta 3 is now available for developers
description: Google has rolled out the third Android 17 beta to developers, about a month after the second. Notably, the first beta was released on February 13. With Beta 3, Android 17 has now reached platform stability. Android 17 Beta 3 is now available for Pixel 6 and newer devices, including the Pixel Tablet and Pixel Fold handsets. Users enrolled in the beta program will receive the update over the air. In an official blog post, Google detailed the new features and improvements in Beta 3, which also includes the March Android security patch. The update introduces support for the...

title: Motorola Razr 70 Ultra CAD renders have leaked
description: The Motorola Razr 70 series is expected to debut in the coming weeks. Recently, the standard Razr 70 surfaced on TENAA, revealing its key specifications. Now, renders of the Razr 70 Ultra have leaked. As per the leak, the Razr 70 Ultra will be slightly thicker than the outgoing Razr 60 Ultra. The upcoming clamshell foldable is said to measure 171.3 x 74.1 x 7.8 mm when unfolded, and 88.0 x 74.1 x 15.8 mm when folded. Meanwhile, the Razr 60 Ultra is 7.2 mm thick in its unfolded state and 15.7 mm when folded. The leak suggests that the slight increase in thickness may be due to...

title: Oppo says the Find X9 Ultra has a built-in 10x teleconverter
description: vivo is launching the X300 Ultra on Monday, and Oppo is working on its very own competitor to that device, the Find X9 Ultra. We already know that it's coming in April, and that, like the X300 Ultra, it will be available globally. Whereas vivo's Ultra has optional teleconverters taking it to high zoom levels, the Oppo Find X9 Ultra will have a built-in 10x telephoto camera. Oppo released a new teaser today calling it a built-in 'teleconverter' of sorts, clearly throwing some shade vivo's way. Oppo Find X9 Ultra 50MP 10x periscope telephoto camera details The Find X9 Ultra has...

title: Google expands AI Mode's Search Live experience to over 200 countries
description: Google announced Search Live in AI Mode in June of last year, and now the feature is expanding to over 200 countries and territories. Basically, it's now available everywhere AI Mode is, and in all languages that AI Mode works in. Search Live is a conversational experience built into Google Search's AI Mode. To enter it, open the Google app on Android or iOS and tap the Live icon under the search bar. Once you do, you can ask questions, get audio responses, then continue the conversation with follow-up questions or dive deeper through web links. You can also ask about...

title: WhatsApp gets multiple account support on iOS, cross-platform transfer from iOS to Android
description: Today, WhatsApp has announced new features, including support for two accounts on the same iPhone. This feature was previously available on Android and has finally made it to iOS. If you use this, you'll always know which account you're in because your profile picture will be visible in the bottom tab. Next up, cross-platform chat transfer now supports moving your history from iOS to Android. You can also find and delete large files directly within a chat - simply tap the chat name and select Manage Storage. You can also choose to clear just media files from any chat, while keeping your...

title: Apple expands its American Manufacturing Program with new partners
description: Back in August of last year, Apple announced its American Manufacturing Program (AMP), with the aim to bring its supply chain and manufacturing partners to US soil. Today, the company has unveiled new members of AMP, including Bosch, Cirrus Logic, TDK, and Qnity Electronics. These join the initial partners: Amkor, Applied Materials, Broadcom, Coherent, Corning, GlobalFoundries, GlobalWafers America, MP Materials, Samsung, and Texas Instruments. TDK will make sensors for Apple in the US for the first time, including for iPhone features like camera stabilization. Bosch and TSMC will...

title: Realme 16 5G is coming to India next month
description: The Realme 16 5G, which debuted in Vietnam in January, has been confirmed to launch in India next month. It features an Air design with a camera bar at the back along with a ‘selfie mirror’. The India-bound variant seems to have the same specs as the global model. Realme has confirmed that the device will launch in India on April 2. It will pack a large 7,000mAh battery within an 8.1mm slim body. The Realme 16 5G is also confirmed to sport a dual rear camera setup with a 50MP primary sensor. One of its standout features is a mirror-like circular surface on the camera bar, which the...

title: Samsung Browser for Windows is now out of beta
description: After five months in beta for Windows, Samsung released the stable version of the Samsung Browser. It can be installed on Windows 10 (version 1809 or higher) and, of course, on Windows 11. The version is 30.0.0.95 and is available globally, unlike the beta variant, which was limited to select markets. As one would expect, Samsung Browser offers seamless cross-device and cross-platform continuity and syncs everything from bookmarks and browsing history to settings and personal data like passwords via the Samsung Pass integration. But that's hardly the centerpiece feature of the...

title: vivo X300 Ultra key specifications revealed ahead of launch
description: Ahead of its launch next week, vivo has been teasing key details about the X300 Ultra and has now confirmed most of the handset’s specifications. The flagship will debut alongside the vivo X300s and the vivo Pad6 Pro. vivo’s product manager Han Boxiao shared the new details on Weibo. The vivo X300 Ultra will be powered by the Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chipset and sport a flat display with a 2K resolution and a 144Hz refresh rate. The handset will offer 100W wired and 40W wireless charging support. Although the battery capacity still hasn’t been confirmed, it is rumored to offer a...

title: Samsung is already working on the Exynos 2800 SoC for the Galaxy S28
description: Samsung launched the Galaxy S26 and Galaxy S26+ recently, and in some markets these are both powered by the company's own Exynos 2600 SoC. Now, a new report from Korea claims Samsung is already working on the Exynos 2800, expected to make its debut in 2028 in the Galaxy S28 family. [#InlinePriceWidget,14456,1#] [#InlinePriceWidget,14457,1#] The Exynos 2800 is rumored to be made by Samsung on a 2nm process (SF2P+, specifically), and Samsung wants to complete the design of the chipset and hand over the schematics to the foundry "within the year". After that step, the foundry will make...

title: DJI Avata 360 drone is here to shoot 360-degree 8K HDR video at 60fps
description: DJI's long-awaited response to Antigravity's A1 is finally here. Meet the DJI Avata 360. As the name implies, it lets you film 360-degree video, in 8K60fps HDR no less, thanks to DJI's flagship 1-inch type sensors with 2.4 μm pixels and high dynamic range. The Avata 360 comes with different lenses that you can use, of which one is the 360-degree lens with 1-inch equivalent sensors. This can also take 120MP photos. Both videos and photos can be exported directly or reframed in post-production. With the Single Lens mode you can use the classic Avata-style filming in 4K60fps. DJI's O4+...
